ip_outcome_note: Gine is a named canonical character in Dragon Ball Super and Dragon Ball Super: Broly. The card name 'Gine, Here to Support' explicitly names her. The vision description — female Saiyan with short dark hair, orange/brown bodysuit, monkey tail, cheerful waving pose — matches Gine's canonical design exactly (she is depicted with a low-class Saiyan combat suit and tail, known for her gentle and warm personality unique among Saiyans). ip_confidence already high, confirmed.

trivia_bullets.text: Gine first appeared in the manga special Dragon Ball Minus: The Departure of the Fated Child (April 2014) before being animated in Dragon Ball Super: Broly (2018). As Goku's mother and Bardock's mate, she is notable as one of the few Saiyans depicted with genuine emotional bonds — her kindness is cited as the source of Goku's own gentle nature. [CBR: Dragon Ball — Everything We Know About Goku's Mother, Gine]
sources: CBR: Dragon Ball — Everything We Know About Goku's Mother, Gine

trivia_bullets.text: BT4-074's oracle text: [Auto] When you attack or combo with this card, if your Leader Card is yellow, place up to 3 cards from the top of your deck in your Drop Area. This milling effect suits Gine's narrative role — a support figure who 'gives up' resources (cards, her son) so others can grow stronger. The 15000 power / 5000 combo statline is standard yellow support. [DBZ Exchange: BT4-074 card listing]

Gine and Bardock are described in Dragon Ball Super as 'among the rare Saiyans who are joined by an emotional bond as opposed to a practical reproductive purpose' — making their relationship canonically unique in a species that Toriyama depicted as broadly indifferent to family. Goku inherited his kindness from her. [Dragon Ball official site: Weekly Character Showcase — Gine]
sources: Dragon Ball official site: Weekly Character Showcase #74 — Gine
